metric_definitions.negotiated_versions.n_with_version: Number of origins in the cipher module results whose natural TLS handshake reported a non-empty negotiated_version value.
metric_definitions.negotiated_versions.version_counts: Counts of negotiated TLS protocol versions for the natural handshake with the cipher module's client, grouped into 'TLSv1.3', 'TLSv1.2', and 'other'.
metric_definitions.tls12_cipher_categories.category_counts: For origins where a forced TLS 1.2 handshake succeeded, counts of tls12_forced_category values: 'recommended' (modern AEAD/ECDHE suites), 'sufficient', 'insecure' (RC4/3DES/SHA-1/CBC families), and 'unknown'.
metric_definitions.tls12_cipher_categories.n_tls12_forced_a: Number of origins where the cipher module successfully forced a TLS 1.2 handshake and recorded a tls12_forced_cipher.
metric_definitions.tls12_weaknesses.n_accepts_insecure_tls1: Number of origins where the INSECURE_CIPHER_STRING_TLS12 probe was attempted and no TLS 1.2 handshake could be completed using those insecure cipher suites.

Number of origins where the cipher module successfully negotiated a TLS 1.2 connection using the INSECURE_CIPHER_STRING_TLS12 bundle (e.g., NULL/EXPORT/RC4/3DES/SHA-1), indicating that the server is willing to accept clearly insecure TLS 1.2 cipher suites.
metric_definitions.tls12_weaknesses.n_allows_cbc_tls12_fals: Number of origins where the CBC-only TLS 1.2 probe was attempted and failed (allows_cbc_tls12=False).
metric_definitions.tls12_weaknesses.n_allows_cbc_tls12_true: Number of origins where the cipher module successfully completed a TLS 1.2 handshake when restricted to CBC-mode cipher suites (allows_cbc_tls12=True).
metric_definitions.tls12_weaknesses.n_allows_sha1_tls12_fal: Number of origins where the SHA-1-only TLS 1.2 probe was attempted and failed (allows_sha1_tls12=False).
metric_definitions.tls12_weaknesses.n_allows_sha1_tls12_tru: Number of origins where the cipher module successfully completed a TLS 1.2 handshake when restricted to SHA-1-based cipher suites (allows_sha1_tls12=True).
metric_definitions.tls12_weaknesses.n_tls12_good_policy: Number of origins where all attempted TLS 1.2 weakness probes (insecure bundle, SHA-1-only, CBC-only) returned False, i.e., every probed weak cipher set was refused.
metric_definitions.tls12_weaknesses.n_tls12_weak_probeable: Number of origins where at least one of the TLS 1.2 weakness probes was attempted (accepts_insecure_tls12, allows_sha1_tls12, allows_cbc_tls12 not all None).
metric_definitions.tls12_weaknesses.pct_tls12_good_policy: Percentage of TLS 1.2–probeable origins (n_tls12_weak_probeable) that refused all probed weak cipher sets (n_tls12_good_policy).
metric_definitions.tls13_cipher_categories.category_counts: For origins where a forced TLS 1.3 handshake succeeded, counts of tls13_forced_category values: 'recommended' (in the CCCS recommended list), 'sufficient' (acceptable but not top-tier), and 'unknown' (not in either list).
metric_definitions.tls13_cipher_categories.n_tls13_forced_a: Number of origins where the cipher module successfully forced a TLS 1.3 handshake and recorded a tls13_forced_cipher.
metric_definitions.tls_overview.n_any_legacy_tls: Number of origins that supported any legacy protocol version (tls11=True, tls10=True, or ssl_legacy=True).
metric_definitions.tls_overview.n_any_modern_tls: Number of origins that supported at least one modern TLS version (tls13=True or tls12=True).
metric_definitions.tls_overview.n_modern_no_legacy: Number of origins that supported at least one modern TLS version (TLS 1.2 or 1.3) and did not support any legacy versions (TLS 1.1, TLS 1.0, SSLv3 all False).
metric_definitions.tls_overview.n_origin_offline_tls: Number of origins where the TLS module recorded an error string containing 'origin offline', indicating that TLS probing was skipped because the origin was already considered offline/unreachable.
metric_definitions.tls_overview.n_origins: Number of origins that appeared in the TLS module results.
metric_definitions.tls_overview.support_counts: For each protocol version (TLS 1.3, TLS 1.2, TLS 1.1, TLS 1.0, SSLv3), the number of origins where the corresponding boolean field (tls13, tls12, tls11, tls10, ssl_legacy) was True, meaning that a handshake using exactly that version succeeded.
metric_definitions.tls_overview.tested_counts: For each protocol version, the number of origins where the corresponding boolean field was not None, i.e., where the client had the capability to probe that version and the scan attempted it (True = supported, False = not supported).
